title: Graphic Designer (E-commerce)
description:
Graphic Designer (E-commerce)

We are looking for a fast, detail-oriented, and highly creative Graphic Designer with experience in e-commerce product image creation and high-volume design production. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a performance-driven environment and can deliver high-quality outputs at scale using AI tools and Adobe Photoshop.



Key Responsibilities:

• Create complete product image sets (6–10 images per SKU) following marketplace standards

• Design campaign banners, promotional graphics, and marketplace visuals

• Produce short product videos (15–60 seconds) for listings and marketing

• Ensure all designs meet platform-specific requirements (Amazon, Kogan, etc.)

• Use AI tools to generate and enhance product images, then perform manual refinements in Adobe Photoshop

• Retouch and correct AI-generated images (lighting, shadows, edges, proportions, colors)

• Maintain consistent branding, layout, and visual quality across all outputs

• Collaborate with listing and marketing teams to meet deadlines





Performance Expectations:

• Deliver high daily output across images, banners, and videos

• Ensure 100% completion of image sets for new listings

• Maintain a minimum 98% quality pass rate (QC standards)

• Meet strict deadlines and platform requirements

• Achieve monthly performance targets based on output points





Qualifications:

• Proven experience in e-commerce graphic design (product images, banners, videos)

• Strong understanding of conversion-focused design

• Experience using AI design tools (background removal, enhancement, automation)

• Proficiency in Adobe Photoshop (required) for editing, retouching, and final quality enhancement

• Experience combining AI-generated outputs with manual Photoshop editing

• Strong eye for detail, able to fix AI imperfections (distorted shapes, shadows, textures)

• Ability to work in a fast-paced, high-volume environment

• Excellent attention to detail and time management



For interested applicant:

Kindly send us a message on Microsoft Teams at 
 and send your CV/resume there so we can discuss further details.

title: Senior UK B2B Lead Researcher (Decision-Maker Data for Sales Outreach)
description:
Applications without clear explanation of their verification process will not be considered



We are a UK-based language services agency specializing in interpreting and translation services for professional organisations.

We are looking for a highly experienced B2B lead researcher to support our sales outreach efforts by building high-quality, verified contact lists of UK decision-makers.

This is a project-based / ongoing role (not full-time) depending on business needs. The data you provide will be used directly for cold calling and personalized
 outreach, so accuracy and quality are extremely important.



Responsibilities:

• Research UK organisations in target sectors such as:

• Law firms (solicitors, legal offices)

• Schools and universities

• Private healthcare clinics

• Mental health services

• Social services and charities

• Construction and corporate services

• Identify real decision-makers responsible for interpreting and translation services, such as:

• Office Managers

• Practice Managers

• Admin Managers

• Procurement Officers

• Operations Managers

• Collect and verify accurate B2B contact data including:

• Company name

• Contact person name

• Job title

• Verified business

• Phone number (direct or switchboard where available)

• Company address

• Ensure data is:

• Up to date

• Verified (not guessed or scraped)

• Relevant to decision-making roles

• Deliver clean, structured spreadsheets ready for sales outreach







 IMPORTANT QUALITY STANDARD:

We are NOT looking for bulk or scraped data.

We only accept verified, real, decision-maker-level leads suitable for cold calling and direct
 outreach.

All data will be checked. Low-quality or unverified data will be rejected.



REQUIREMENTS:

• Minimum 3–5+ years’ experience in B2B lead generation or data research

• Proven experience sourcing decision-maker contacts (not generic databases)

• Strong understanding of UK business structures

• Experience using:

• LinkedIn

• Google Sheets

•
 verification / enrichment tools (e.g. Hunter, Apollo, etc.)

• Ability to verify contact accuracy (not just collect data)

• Strong attention to detail

• Fluent English comprehension

• Able to work independently with minimal supervision





WORK SETUP:

• Remote

• Project-based / ongoing assignments

• Flexible hours

• Work assigned based on business needs



PAYMENT:

We are open to one of the following structures:

• Per 1,000 verified decision-maker leads

• Hourly rate (for highly experienced professionals only)

• Performance-based bonuses for high accuracy and low bounce rate

Final rate will depend on experience and quality of data delivered.



 TO APPLY:

Please include:

• CV / work history

• Examples of previous B2B lead generation work

• Tools used for research and verification

• Explanation of your verification process (very important)

Bonus if you can describe how you ensure:

 “decision-maker accuracy +
 validity”

title: VIDEO EDITOR FOR COURSE CONTENT
description:
WHAT I NEED:

I have 32 raw video modules (5-15 minutes each) for an online cold calling masterclass. Currently, they're just talking head footage.

I need you to transform them into professional course content with:

1. Loom-style layout (talking head in corner + visual workspace)

2. Diagrams/visual explanations overlaid while I'm explaining concepts

3. On-screen text for key points, frameworks, statistics

4. End-of-module summary screens ("What We Learned" with bullet points)

5. Basic editing (remove pauses, filler words, clean audio)

________________________________________

EXAMPLE OF WHAT I WANT:

Think Loom screen recordings where:

• My talking head is in a circle/square in the corner (20-25% of screen)

• The main screen shows diagrams, frameworks, text, illustrations

• Key points appear as text overlays

• Each module ends with a summary slide listing what was covered

Reference style: Loom tutorials, Kajabi course videos, Alex Hormozi's course content

________________________________________

SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS:

Per module, you'll need to:

• Watch raw footage (5-15 min each)

• Create 3-8 diagrams/visual aids based on what I'm teaching

• Add Loom-style layout (me in corner, diagrams on main screen)

• Add on-screen text for key points (frameworks, stats, quotes)

• Remove filler words, long pauses, "ums"

• Create end-of-module summary screen (bullet point list of what was learned)

• Export in 1080p MP4

Diagrams needed for:

• The 5-stage call structure

• Objection handling frameworks

• Psychological principles (cognitive dissonance, social proof, etc.)

• Industry application examples

• Activity tracking sheets

• The sales machine (3 levers framework)

You don't need to animate - simple, clean diagrams are fine. Think whiteboard-style or slide deck visuals.

________________________________________

WHAT YOU SHOULD HAVE:

? Experience editing course content (show examples)

? Ability to create simple diagrams/graphics (Canva, Illustrator, PowerPoint, whatever works)

? Video editing software (Premiere, Final Cut, CapCut, DaVinci Resolve - doesn't matter which)

? Fast turnaround (ideally 2-3 modules per day once we're rolling)

? Good communication (I'll provide notes on what diagrams to create)

? Access to stock graphics/icons (or ability to create simple ones)

________________________________________

WHAT I'LL PROVIDE:

• 32 raw video files (talking head footage)

• Written notes on what each module covers

• Brand colors/fonts (for consistency)

• Examples of diagrams I want (I'll sketch rough versions)

• Feedback on first 2-3 modules to establish style

________________________________________

DELIVERABLES:

• 32 edited course modules (5-15 min each)

• 1080p MP4 format

• Organized file naming (Module 01 - What Closing Actually Is.mp4)

• Source files (in case we need to make changes later)

________________________________________

TIMELINE:

• Test edit: 2 modules first (to confirm style/quality)

• Bulk editing: Remaining 30 modules over 2-3 weeks

• Final deadline: April 30, 2026

________________________________________

TO APPLY:

Send me:

1. Portfolio - Show me 2-3 examples of course videos you've edited (especially if you've done Loom-style or diagram overlays)

2. Your rate - Quote for all 32 modules OR per-module rate

3. Timeline - How many modules can you edit per day?

4. Tools you use - What software do you edit in?

5. Sample work - If you don't have course content examples, show me ANY video where you've added diagrams/graphics overlaid on talking head footage

Please include the word "FRAMEWORK" in your application so I know you read this.

________________________________________

title: LINKEDIN MANAGER & CONTENT REPURPOSER
description:
JOB TITLE: LinkedIn Manager - Grow Pseudonymous Account & Repurpose
 Content

Type: Freelance / Ongoing (3-6 month contract, renewable)

Budget: £200-400/month (negotiate based on scope)

Hours: 5-10 hours/week

Platform: Remote

________________________________________

WHAT I NEED:

I run a personal brand called The Geo Method (cold calling & sales training for high-ticket B2B).

I have an active
/X account with regular content. I need someone to:

1. Create a pseudonymous LinkedIn account called "Geo Method" (NOT my real name - this is a separate brand account)

2. Repurpose my
 content for LinkedIn (adapt tone, format, style for LinkedIn audience)

3. Grow the LinkedIn account using organic tactics (commenting, engaging, posting consistently)

4. Post 3-5 times per week (mix of repurposed
 content + original LinkedIn posts)

5. Engage with my target audience (sales directors, business owners, financial services professionals)

________________________________________

YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES:

Content Creation (70% of role):

• Review my
 posts weekly

• Select 3-5 best-performing tweets

• Rewrite them for LinkedIn (longer form, more professional tone, case studies, storytelling)

• Format with line breaks, emojis, hooks (LinkedIn-specific best practices)

• Create LinkedIn carousels occasionally (using Canva or similar)

• Write original LinkedIn posts when
 content doesn't translate

Account Growth (20% of role):

• Comment on posts from target audience (sales leaders, business owners, cold calling posts)

• Engage with relevant hashtags (#sales #coldcalling #b2bsales)

• Connect with 20-30 relevant people per week (personalized connection requests)

• DM strategy for warm connections (if appropriate)

Reporting (10% of role):

• Weekly report: posts published, engagement stats, follower growth

• Monthly strategy session (what's working, what to adjust)

________________________________________

EXAMPLE OF WHAT YOU'LL DO:

My
 post: "Stop saying 'does that make sense?' You sound weak. Say this instead: [framework]. 47% of salespeople do this. Don't be one of them."

Your LinkedIn adaptation: "I've analyzed over 10,000 sales calls. 47% of salespeople unconsciously sabotage themselves with one phrase: 'Does that make sense?'

Here's why it kills deals:

When you ask for validation after making a point, you're handing the prospect your scorecard. You're signaling that you need THEIR approval to feel confident in your own expertise.

The human brain is wired to follow certainty. When someone speaks with conviction, we trust them. When someone immediately checks if we agree, we start to doubt them.

What to do instead:

Make your point. Then STOP. Let it land. Don't seek validation.

If you want their input, ask from curiosity: 'What are your thoughts on that?'

Not: 'Does that make sense?' (seeking approval) But: 'What's your take?' (inviting perspective)

One signals weakness. The other signals confidence.

I've trained over 1,000 salespeople on this framework. The ones who master it see 20-40% close rate improvements within weeks.

________________________________________

What's one phrase you've eliminated from your sales calls? Drop it in the comments."

See the difference?
 = punchy, short, direct. LinkedIn = storytelling, longer, case study, engagement ask.

________________________________________

WHAT YOU SHOULD HAVE:

? LinkedIn expertise - You understand LinkedIn algorithm, best practices, content formats

? Copywriting skills - You can adapt short
 posts into longer LinkedIn storytelling

? Sales/B2B knowledge - You understand sales terminology (or can learn quickly)

? Content repurposing experience - You've done this before 
 ? LinkedIn or similar)

? Growth tactics - You know how to organically grow LinkedIn accounts (not spammy tactics)

? Canva skills - Can create simple carousels/graphics for LinkedIn posts

? Availability - 1-2 hours per day, Monday-Friday

________________________________________

BONUS SKILLS (NOT REQUIRED):

• Experience with sales/B2B/financial services content

• Familiarity with personal branding on LinkedIn

• Previous work with thought leaders or coaches

• Video editing (for LinkedIn video posts later)

________________________________________

WHAT I'LL PROVIDE:

• Access to my
 account (to see content)

• Brand guidelines (tone, voice, formatting)

• Weekly call for 30 min (strategy, feedback, planning)

• Content calendar (topics I want covered)

• LinkedIn account access (once created)

________________________________________

DELIVERABLES:

Weekly:

• 3-5 LinkedIn posts (scheduled via Buffer or native LinkedIn)

• 50-100 comments on relevant posts

• 20-30 new connection requests sent

• Engagement report (what got most traction)

Monthly:

• Growth report (followers, post impressions, engagement rate)

• Content analysis (what worked, what didn't)

• Strategy recommendations for next month

________________________________________

TIMELINE:

• Week 1: Create LinkedIn account, set up profile, initial strategy call

• Week 2-4: Post 3x/week, grow account, test content formats

• Month 2+: Scale to 5x/week, optimize based on what's working

________________________________________

TO APPLY:

Send me:

1. Portfolio - Show me 2-3 LinkedIn accounts you've managed (or content you've created for LinkedIn)

2. Sample repurpose - Take ONE of my tweets (I'll provide) and rewrite it for LinkedIn to show me your style

title: Advanced GoHighLevel SDR / Diagnostic Advisor — Home Services (EST Hours)
description:
Window Restoration Experts (Toronto, Canada) is hiring a Diagnostic Advisor SDR to own the front end of our sales process. This is NOT a generic appointment-setter role — you will be the qualification filter that protects our field team's calendar and the homeowner's experience.



ABOUT US

We are a premium window restoration company operating across the GTA. Our model is "Restore First. Replace When Right." — we diagnose before we sell, and we walk away from leads that aren't a fit. Our SDR mantra: "I don't convert everyone. I identify who is ready."



WHAT YOU'LL DO

- Handle 40-60 inbound and outbound leads per week via phone, SMS, and form fills

- Run structured discovery calls using our 10-step Advisor Flow (full playbook provided)

- Score every lead 1-5 using our qualification rubric (intent, urgency, timeline, willingness to follow process)

- Book qualified assessments directly into our field team's Jobber calendar

- Use GoHighLevel daily — contacts, opportunities, pipelines, tags, workflows, conversation AI

- Communicate in Slack with the closer, ops, and owner — proactive, clean, coherent

- Handle the $175 assessment-fee conversation with confidence (we never discount)

- Execute clean disqualification when a lead isn't aligned — no chasing



WHAT YOU MUST BRING

- 2+ years advanced GoHighLevel experience (must have built workflows, pipelines, custom fields, and configured conversation AI — not just "used GHL")

- Client intake or appointment-setting track record for North American home services or high-ticket services (roofing, HVAC, solar, remodeling, etc.)

- English fluency at an ADVISORY tone — crisp, calm, confident. You'll be on the phone with Toronto homeowners daily.

- Experience with Jobber, ServiceTitan, Housecall Pro, or similar field-service CRM

- Slack-native communicator

- Comfortable working 9am-5pm EST (10pm-6am Manila) — graveyard, full time

- Coachable, humble, and willing to be trained via Loom and live role-plays by our COO

- Stable internet, quiet home office, good-quality headset



BONUS POINTS

- Prior experience working for Toronto / EST Canadian companies (retention signal)

- Experience with Perspective Funnel or similar funnel builders

- History of pushing back on your manager when you thought you were right



HOW WE WORK

- Full onboarding with our COO — SOPs, role-plays, and a 2-4 week shadow period with our closer before you take the queue solo

- Weekly 1:1 coaching with the closer

- Monthly calibration calls with the owner

- You'll have a real voice on process improvements — we want a thinker, not a task-taker



COMPENSATION

$10-$14/hr USD depending on experience, paid bi-weekly. 40 hours/week. Performance-based raises reviewed at 90 days and 6 months.



HOW TO APPLY

Please include:

1. A 2-3 minute Loom video introducing yourself AS IF you were calling a Toronto homeowner for the first time about foggy windows. No script-reading — be yourself.

2. A written response to this question (80 words max): "A homeowner says $175 is too much for an assessment. How do you respond?"

3. List every GoHighLevel workflow or automation you have personally built, with the company name.

4. Your experience with Jobber or equivalent field-service CRMs.

5. Your current or most recent graveyard schedule and how long you've sustained it.



Applications without the Loom video will not be reviewed.

description:
We are a luxury real estate media company seeking a full-time Senior Photo Editor with deep expertise in real estate and architectural photography. About the Role

As our full-time editor, you will be responsible for editing a steady volume of high-end residential and architectural images. Every image must reflect true-to-life color, clean verticals, natural lighting, and refined contrast.

This is not a preset-driven or automated role—we prioritize precision, consistency, and craftsmanship.



Required Expertise

8+ years of professional photo editing experience (senior level)

Strong background in:

* Real estate photography editing

* Architectural and editorial-style photography

* Advanced expertise in:

* Flambient editing (flash + ambient blending)

Hand-blended HDR (manual only)

Deep understanding of:

* Natural color balance and lighting consistency

* Perspective correction and vertical alignment

* Window pulls and exposure blending

* Clean, high-end editorial finishing



Technical Requirements

Advanced proficiency in:

* Adobe Lightroom Classic

* Adobe Photoshop

Strictly no use of AI-based or automated editing tools, including:

* Fotello

AutoHDR platforms

* Ability to maintain a consistent editing style across large volumes while applying judgment per image



What We’re Looking For

A dedicated full-time editor—not a freelancer juggling multiple clients

Someone who understands light, composition, and realism—not just presets

Strong attention to detail (no halos, color casts, or over-processing)

Reliable communication and fast, consistent turnaround times

* Ability to align with a luxury brand aesthetic



Nice to Have

* Experience editing luxury listings ($1M+ properties)

* Familiarity with U.S. real estate photography standards

title: Video Editor for Weekly YouTube Channel (must understand retention & hooks)
description:
About the channel:

I run a small YouTube channel (6000+ subscribers) focused on classical yoga (in the tradition of Sadhguru), coaching, identity work, emotional intelligence, and personal transformation for ambitious professionals and entrepreneurs.





What I'm looking for:

A long-term collaboration with a skilled, reliable video editor to produce one fully edited video per week (with a minimum of 2–3 videos per month). You will handle the complete editing process from raw footage to final upload-ready video.





Yourresponsibilities include:

- Editing a strong, scroll-stopping intro (first 90 seconds to 3 minutes) — this is the most critical part of each video and where most editing effort goes

- Creating re-hooks between chapters — short visual or editorial moments (B-roll transitions, jump cuts, text elements) designed to maintain viewer attention and reduce drop-off

- Cutting out verbal mistakes and filler (I typically say "okay, let's do this one again" on camera to mark retakes)

- Selecting and placing B-roll footage (from a provided personal collection and from HiggsField, an AI tool we subscribe to for generating custom images and video clips)

- Selecting background music that fits the mood and content

- Adding text overlays such as chapter titles, key terms, and references where indicated

- Editing guided meditation or practice segments, which typically involve a static screen with background music





How we collaborate:

For each video, I share either the full script or a detailed outline as a document. I use comments within the document to mark where specific text overlays, chapter headings, references, or particular B-roll should be inserted. This gives you clear structure and minimizes back-and-forth.

Once we're in sync, very few revision rounds should be needed. During the initial onboarding phase, some additional iterations are expected and completely normal.





Time allocation:

Editing each video will likely take approximately 6–10 hours to edit depending on complexity (usually 40 minutes of raw video with fillers / gaps into 25 minutes of final cut). Most of the editing time is spent on the intro section; the remainder of the video tends to follow a more consistent structure. 





Reference videos:

Please review these three sample videos to understand the style, pacing, and quality level we expect:













How to apply:

Before we begin working together, I'd like to see a 30-90 second edited intro based on a raw video file and its script (see below).

Please deliver the result as a Google Drive link. This gives both of us a clear picture of fit and quality before committing.

No need to invest more than 1 hour for this.





Here's the input for the trial intro:

- raw files in German (video):





- raw file with English voiceover:





- script with notes on what to edit:





Once we start working together, I'll also provide:

--> Personal B-roll collection (access granted once collaboration starts)

--> Higgs Field subscription for generating custom AI images and video B-roll





I look forward to finding a long-term editing partner!

If you have experience with coaching, wellness, or educational YouTube content, that's a plus — but strong editing instincts and reliability matter most.

title: Social Media Support and Content Editing
description:
Virtual Assistant – Social Media & Content Support

Part-Time | Remote | ~8–10 hours per week



Overview

Sleepyhead Studios (
) is hiring a Virtual Assistant to support social media growth and content editing for a professional music producer and mix engineer. The role focuses on short-form video editing and social media discovery tasks within the online music production and audio engineering community.



The assistant will help identify engagement opportunities, mine audience questions, upload content, and edit short-form videos.



The VA will like posts for engagement but will not comment publicly on behalf of the account. Instead, the assistant will collect posts for the account owner to review and comment on.



The role will start with approximately 7 videos per week, with the potential to increase to up to 14 videos per week beginning around July.



Sleepyhead Studios will also be releasing music production software in July. At that time, video editing and engagement needs will increase. The goal is to have a VA in place by May in order to build an efficient workflow before the workload increases.



Core Responsibilities

1. Short-Form Video Editing: Edit vertical short-form videos for social media.

Initial volume: ~7 videos per week

Potential future volume in 1-2 months: up to 14 videos per week



Editing tasks

trim dead space

add subtitles/captions

apply jump cuts where needed

add light zoom cuts for pacing

export vertical format (9:16)



2. Engagement Post Discovery: Identify posts where the account owner should engage.

Two categories must be collected each week.

Artist / Producer Posts

Posts from:

artists showcasing their music

producers showing their beats or songs

Deliverable:

15 posts per week including: creator name, link to the post, and a short note explaining why it’s a good post to engage with

The VA may like these posts but should not comment.

Music Creator Posts

Posts from creators discussing the craft of production or mixing.

Target accounts include:

mix engineers

producer educators

producer creators discussing workflow, mixing, gear, or production techniques

Deliverable:

15 posts per week including: creator name, link to the post, short description of the post topic

The VA may like these posts but should not comment.



3. Comment Mining (Audience Questions): Collect useful questions from comments on music production or mixing posts.

Focus on questions related to:

mixing issues

production workflow

plugin usage

sound design

beginner production or mixing problems



Deliverable: 10–20 comments per week that could inspire content ideas.



4. Content Posting / Repurposing: Upload finished videos onto Instagram Reels, TikTok. YouTube Shorts

Tasks include:

uploading videos

inserting captions

adding hashtags

selecting a thumbnail frame



5. Weekly Analytics Summary: Provide a short weekly report tracking views, likes, saves, comments, follower growth



Estimated Weekly Workload

Video editing (7 videos): ~3–4 hours

Engagement discovery: ~2 hours

Comment mining: ~45 minutes

Content posting: ~1–1.5 hours

Analytics: ~30 minutes

Estimated total: ~8–10 hours per week with potential of 15-20 hours by July.





Security Requirements

The assistant must:

use a VPN set to the United States when accessing social media accounts

use login credentials provided by the company only for work purposes





Requirements

Candidates should:

have experience editing short-form video

understand social media platforms (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts)

be comfortable researching creators in the music niche

be organized and detail-oriented



Preferred:

interest in music production and/or audio engineering

familiarity with producer or mixing communities online



Compensation:

Starting hourly rate of $3 to $5 based on experience.

Starting workload: 8–10 hours per week.

The role will expand to 15-20 hours per week as video volume increases and the software launch approaches.





Test Task: Applicants will be given a short raw vertical video clip. The applicant must edit it into a short-form social media video.



The final edit should include:

dead space removed

clear pacing with jump cuts

subtitles/captions added

light zoom cuts for visual movement

final export in vertical 9:16 format

Please reference these TikToks to see how I like my videos edited. I am open to all ideas on how to make the video edits more engaging.

title: Senior AI Systems Engineer (Real-Time Voice + Agent Architecture)
description:
We are building a high-performance AI operating system (LifeOS) with real-time execution.



This is NOT a chatbot project.



We are looking for a senior engineer capable of building a real-time AI pipeline with voice interaction and multi-agent routing.



CORE REQUIREMENTS:



1. Voice pipeline (REAL-TIME)

- microphone input

- streaming STT (speech-to-text)

- real-time processing through AI engine

- response generation

- streaming TTS (text-to-speech)



2. Agent routing (CRITICAL)

- ATHENA (Individual) ? must ALWAYS be used for Individual

- TARA (Ventures) ? must ALWAYS be used for Ventures

- NO cross-routing

- must be enforced at backend level (NOT UI)



3. Execution layer

- real actions (calendar, integrations)

- create / update / delete (real API execution)

- NO mock or fake execution



4. Performance

- real-time streaming

- low latency

- stable system



TECH STACK (flexible, but must justify):

- Python / Node.js

- FastAPI / WebSockets

- OpenAI / Claude

- STT: Whisper / Deepgram / Google

- TTS: ElevenLabs / Azure

- Redis / event-driven architecture



IMPORTANT:

- Senior level only

- You must have built real-time systems before

- No junior / no basic AI tools experience



TO APPLY — REQUIRED:

Answer these 3 questions:



1. How would you build a real-time voice pipeline (mic ? STT ? AI ? TTS)?

2. How do you enforce strict backend routing between multiple agents?

3. How do you reduce latency in a streaming AI system?



We are moving fast and expect production-level execution.



No partial delivery. Only complete systems.

description:
We are Sovereign, a brand growth agency based in Dubai. We scale the brands of influencers and founders who have an audience but no system to turn it into business growth. We do this through content and Meta Ads — and we need a video editor who understands what makes people stop scrolling.

This is a long-term, part-time role with the opportunity to grow into full-time as we scale.

What you will do:

Work from raw footage and a clear creative brief — no guessing

Add captions, hooks, transitions, music, and text overlays that match the brand tone

Deliver clean, scroll-stopping content consistently and on time

Take feedback well and implement it fast



What we need from you:



Minimum 2 years of experience editing short-form social media content

A portfolio of at least 5 real Reels or TikToks you have edited — no portfolio means no interview, no exceptions

Excellent written English

Clear briefs, no chaos, no last-minute changes

A long-term role with a fast-growing agency

Room to grow as we add more clients

Start your application with the word SOVEREIGN. Then tell us in two sentences what makes your editing style different from other editors. After that, include a link to your portfolio — Google Drive, YouTube, TikTok, or any link that works. Applications that do not start with SOVEREIGN will not be read.

title: Mobile App Developer
description:
We are looking for a talented and passionate Mobile App Developer to join our growing engineering team. In this role, you will design, build, and maintain high-quality mobile applications for both Android and iOS platforms. You will collaborate closely with product managers, designers, and backend engineers to deliver exceptional user experiences to our customers.

Key Responsibilities

• Design and develop robust, scalable, and maintainable Android (Kotlin/Java) and iOS (Swift) applications

• Collaborate with UX/UI designers to translate wireframes and mockups into polished, pixel-perfect interfaces

• Integrate RESTful APIs, third-party SDKs, and cloud services into mobile applications

• Write clean, well-documented, and testable code following established best practices

• Conduct code reviews and provide constructive feedback to peers

• Optimize application performance, responsiveness, and battery efficiency

• Troubleshoot, debug, and resolve bugs and performance bottlenecks

• Stay up to date with the latest mobile development trends, tools, and technologies

• Participate in Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, standups, and retrospectives

• Publish and maintain apps on the Google Play Store and Apple App Store

Required Qualifications

• 3+ years of professional mobile development experience on Android and/or iOS

• Proficiency in Kotlin or Java for Android development

• Proficiency in Swift for iOS development

• Solid understanding of mobile UI/UX principles, Material Design, and Apple Human Interface Guidelines

• Experience with RESTful API integration, JSON parsing, and networking libraries (Retrofit, Alamofire, etc.)

• Familiarity with version control systems, particularly Git

• Experience with unit testing and UI testing frameworks (JUnit, Espresso, XCTest)

• Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail

• Excellent communication skills and ability to work effectively in a collaborative team environment

Nice to Have

• Experience with cross-platform frameworks such as Flutter or React Native

• Knowledge of CI/CD pipelines and mobile DevOps practices (Fastlane, Bitrise, GitHub Actions)

• Familiarity with Firebase, push notifications, and analytics SDKs

• Experience with offline-first architecture and local data persistence (Room, CoreData, SQLite)

• Prior experience publishing and maintaining apps with 100k+ active users

• Contributions to open-source mobile projects

title: Remote Operations & Systems Coordinator

As we continue to scale, we are looking for someone who thrives behind the scenes—bringing structure, clarity, and efficiency into a fast-moving environment.

Clean up and build Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)

Track financial activity across projects

Improve team accountability and workflows

Ensure nothing falls through the cracks

Key Responsibilities



Systems & SOP Development



Document and organize current workflows into clear SOPs

Identify inefficiencies and recommend improvements

Maintain and update systems as the company evolves



Financial & Project Tracking



Track expenses for each project

Monitor budgets and flag inconsistencies

Ensure accurate record-keeping across all jobs



Operations & Accountability



Verify employee hours and time tracking

Support onboarding systems for new hires

Maintain organized files, folders, and internal documentation



Continuous Improvement



Identify gaps, delays, or inefficiencies

Proactively suggest solutions

Help implement better processes over time

What We’re Looking For

Strong attention to detail (nothing slips past you)

Experience with operations, systems, or project coordination

Comfortable working independently and taking initiative

Strong organizational and problem-solving skills

Experience with tools like Google Workspace, Excel, and project management platforms (Asana, Trello, etc.)

Clear and professional communication



Bonus (not required):



Experience in construction or project-based businesses

Experience with bookkeeping or cost tracking



What Success Looks Like

Operations become more organized and structured

Financial tracking is clear and consistent

Team accountability improves

Systems run smoothly without constant oversight



How to Apply



Please complete the application form below and answer all questions honestly and thoughtfully:





Attention to detail is important in this role. Please include the word “structure” somewhere in your assessment.



Final Note



We are looking for someone who takes pride in being the person who keeps everything running smoothly behind the scenes. If you value organization, clarity, and continuous improvement, we’re excited to hear from you.

title: Web Developer (WordPress & Hosting Specialist)
description:
We are looking for a reliable and detail-oriented Web Developer to manage the technical lifecycle of our web projects. This isn't just about writing code; it’s about ensuring our digital infrastructure is stable, fast, and visually compelling. You will be our "go-to" expert for site migrations, DNS management, and building high-converting pages using Elementor.



As this is an hourly contract role, we value efficiency, clear communication, and the ability to troubleshoot independently.



Key Responsibilities

Infrastructure Management: Take full ownership of DNS configurations, records (A, CNAME, MX, TXT), and SSL certifications.

Site Migrations: Execute seamless website migrations between hosting providers (e.g., SiteGround, WP Engine, AWS) with zero to minimal downtime.

Page Building: Design, build, and deploy responsive, high-performance landing pages using Elementor Pro and WordPress.

Performance Optimization: Audit and improve site speed (Core Web Vitals), optimize imagery, and manage plugin bloat.

Maintenance & Security: Perform regular updates, backups, and security hardening to ensure site integrity.

Troubleshooting: Resolve "white screen of death" errors, CSS conflicts, and hosting-level bottlenecks.



Technical Requirements

WordPress Expertise: Deep knowledge of the WordPress ecosystem, specifically theme customization and Elementor page building.

Server & Hosting Savvy: Proven experience with cPanel, FTP/SFTP, and various hosting environments.

DNS Proficiency: You should be able to explain the difference between a Nameserver change and an A-record update in your sleep.

Front-End Basics: Proficiency in CSS/HTML to customize what Elementor can’t do out-of-the-box.

Attention to Detail: A "pixel-perfect" mindset—ensuring designs are consistent across all breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop).



Preferred Qualifications

Experience with WP Rocket, Cloudflare, or other caching/CDN tools.

Understanding of SEO best practices as they relate to site structure and speed.

Basic knowledge of PHP for minor theme file edits.



Please submit your resume and a brief portfolio or list of live sites you have managed/developed.

title: Accounting Operations Manager
description:
THE CHIC CFO

 



Accounting Operations Manager

Remote  |  Full-Time or Part-Time  |  US-Based or Fluent English Required



About The Chic CFO

The Chic CFO is a fractional CFO and bookkeeping firm that helps small business owners — especially cleaning company owners — take control of their finances. We don't just categorize transactions. We deliver custom financial dashboards, clear reporting, and the kind of financial clarity that helps our clients know exactly what to pay themselves, eliminate tax surprises, and design the life they actually want.



We're a small, high-performing remote team and we're growing. We're looking for a sharp, client-facing Accounting Operations Manager to join us and take ownership of both our client relationships and our bookkeeping team's day-to-day performance.



The Role

This is not a traditional bookkeeping position. You will be the operational and client-facing backbone of The Chic CFO. You'll lead new client onboarding, serve as the primary point of contact for our clients, manage and quality-check our bookkeeping team's work, and make sure every deliverable goes out accurately and on time.



You need to be as comfortable on a client call as you are inside QuickBooks Online. You are organized, proactive, and you hold yourself and others to a high standard.



What You'll Be Responsible For

Client Relationship Management

• Lead new client onboarding calls and walk clients through our process from day one

• Serve as the primary point of contact for ongoing client communication

• Respond to client questions professionally and in a timely manner

• Build strong relationships that reduce churn and increase client satisfaction

Bookkeeping Team Oversight

• Manage and support our bookkeeping team, ensuring all work meets our quality standards

• Review completed bookkeeping work before it goes to clients

• Identify errors, inconsistencies, or missing items and ensure they are corrected

• Hold the team accountable to deadlines and deliverable timelines

• Provide feedback and direction to bookkeepers as needed

Operations & Deliverables

• Own the client deliverables calendar and ensure nothing falls through the cracks

• Monitor the status of all active client accounts on an ongoing basis

• Step in to assist with bookkeeping when workload requires it

• Support process improvement initiatives as the team grows

 



What We're Looking For

Must-Haves

• 3+ years of bookkeeping or accounting experience

• Proficient in QuickBooks Online (QBO certification is a strong plus)

• Experience in a client-facing role — you are comfortable on the phone and over video

• Strong written and verbal English communication skills

• Experience reviewing or overseeing another person's work

• Highly organized with excellent follow-through

• Able to work US business hours (Eastern or Central time)

Nice-to-Haves

• Experience working at a bookkeeping firm, accounting agency, or fractional CFO company

• Familiarity with Dubsado, Looker Studio, Asana, or similar tools

• Experience working with service-based small businesses (cleaning companies a major plus)

• Comfortable building or reviewing financial reports and dashboards

 



Compensation

Compensation is based on experience and location. This role starts at approximately 30 hours per week with the potential to grow to full-time as the business scales.



How to Apply

To be considered, please submit the following:

• A 60–90 second Loom video introducing yourself and briefly describing your bookkeeping and client-facing experience

• Your resume

• A note on your current availability and preferred hours

Applications without a Loom video will not be reviewed.

We are excited to find the right person to grow with us. If you are detail-oriented, love working with clients, and want to be part of a brand that is genuinely changing how small business owners relate to their money — we would love to hear from you.

title: Creative Growth Operator — Social Media, Content Strategy & Video Editing
description:
We are hiring a full-time Creative Growth Operator to help grow a founder-led personal brand in the business/consulting space.

The goal is simple:

Research what's working. Create content that stops the scroll. Grow the audience. Turn that audience into inbound leads.

This role covers the full content operation — from concept to publish. If you only edit videos and wait to be told what to make, this is not for you.

You need to think like a growth operator and execute like a content team of one.



What You'll Own End to End

Strategy & Research



Research viral content formats and trending videos in the business/consulting space each week

Adapt trending formats for our specific audience (service business owners — tradies, mortgage brokers, physios, real estate agents)

Pitch 2–3 content ideas per week unprompted — come with a "you have to do this one" attitude

Identify what's performing on the account and double down on it

Track weekly performance (views, reach, engagement, follower growth) and report findings



Content Production



Script talking head reels, B-roll style videos, and carousels

Edit short-form videos for Instagram Reels and YouTube Shorts — tight pacing, captions, hooks

Repurpose long-form YouTube videos into multiple short clips

Design and produce carousel posts from scratch

Create engaging thumbnails for YouTube



Social Media Execution



Upload and schedule all content across Instagram and YouTube

Write captions in the founder's voice

Manage YouTube uploads (titles, descriptions, tags)

Post daily Instagram Stories — polls, lead magnet pushes, behind the scenes

Start DMs and reply to comments to drive engagement



Paid Ads (Ideal but not essential)



Assist with Meta ad creative — cut and prepare video/image assets to spec

Help write ad copy using a structured template

Flag when creatives are fatiguing and suggest replacements





Who This Is For



You understand hooks, retention, and what makes someone stop scrolling

You actively study creators and trends — you know what's going viral before being told

You can take an idea from concept to published post without hand-holding

You're fast. You finish things. You don't need chasing.

You have strong English — written and spoken

You want long-term work and will grow with the role

Experience with Meta Ads is a bonus — not required





Application Instructions

To apply, answer all of the following:



Send 2–3 examples of short-form content you've produced — ideally where you were involved in the concept, not just the edit

Find a video that's currently going viral in the business or personal brand space. Tell us why it's working and how you'd recreate it for an Australian business consultant

What makes someone stop scrolling in the first 2 seconds? Give us your answer, not a generic one

Have you worked with an Australian-based client or business before? (If not, that's ok)

Have you ever managed a social media account end to end — not just edited the videos?

What tools do you use for video editing, scheduling, and content research?

How would you grow a personal brand Instagram account from 20K to 100K followers in 12 months? Give us your actual thinking.

title: Video Editor & TikTok Growth Specialist
description:
We are looking for a creative, fast-moving video editor who lives and breathes 

short-form content. You'll receive raw footage filmed by me and turn it into 

polished, high-performing TikTok videos, optimized for organic growth and 

built to complement our paid ad campaigns.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

- Edit raw footage into scroll-stopping TikTok videos (hooks, pacing, captions, 

  transitions, trending audio)

- Optimize every video for TikTok's algorithm, proper length, format, 

  on-screen text, and CTAs

- Repurpose top-performing TikTok content into Instagram Reels and YouTube 

  Shorts as needed

- Coordinate with the paid ads strategy, ensure organic content and ad 

  creatives are visually consistent and reinforce the same message

- Stay current on TikTok trends, sounds, and formats and proactively suggest 

  content angles

- Deliver a consistent posting schedule (we will align on volume per week)

- Provide basic performance feedback, flag which videos are gaining traction 

  so we can double down



REQUIREMENTS:

- Strong portfolio of TikTok or short-form video edits, REQUIRED (please 

  include links)

- Proficient in CapCut, Opus Clip, etc

- Deep understanding of TikTok hooks, retention, and what drives shares/saves

- Experience editing content that runs alongside paid ads (Meta or TikTok Ads)

  is a strong plus

- Fast turnaround, ideally 24-hour edit cycles

- Good communication and ability to take direction on brand voice and messaging

- Experience in personal brand, coaching, finance, or service-based niches 

  is a bonus

-must send 2-3 tik toks edited from raw footage if application is to be considered.



This is a part-time role with the potential to grow to full-time as content 

volume increases. If you have an eye for what makes people stop scrolling and 

take action, we want to work with you.

title: Full-Time YouTube Video Editor (Faceless Channels) – $30 per Video + Paid Test Project
description:
Please read in full before responding or your application will not be considered. 



We are launching new YouTube faceless channels and currently testing multiple niches (facts, top lists, motivational, finance, health, and others). We have a large bank of videos ready to produce and are looking to hire 3 skilled video editors to join our remote team on a full-time basis.



You will receive the script and voiceover tool (Eleven Labs). Experience with ElevenLabs is a big plus.



Pay:



$30 per completed 30 minute video (equivalent to ~$1 per minute of final edited video)

Paid test project: $10 for editing a 1-minute sample video (payment will be immediate via paypal)

Consistent full-time workload with long-term opportunity. 

Weekly pay for all videos completed that week. (The faster you can complete high quality videos, the more videos we can assign you.) 



Bonuses

You will receive bonuses for the performance of the videos as follows.

100,000 views: +$25 bonus

500,000 views: +$50 additional bonus (total $75)

1,000,000 views: +$200 additional bonus (total $275 maximum per video)

$20 bonus for completing 3 videos in one week. Totalling $80/month if you do this every week for an entire month. 

This puts your on track earnings at roughly $400-$600/Month if you can produce 12 videos per month and reach a couple of view bonuses. 



Responsibilities:



Source your own relevant stock footage(we have a team stock footage subscription to Envanto as well) , B-roll, and images based on the script

Edit 30 minute engaging faceless YouTube videos

Add smooth cuts, dynamic transitions, text overlays, subtitles/captions, zoom/pan effects (Ken Burns), sound design, and background music

Color correct footage, enhance audio, and optimize pacing for high viewer retention

Deliver polished, YouTube-ready videos with proper organization

Complete each 30 minute video on a strict 48 hour timeline, this is non negotiable if you want the position. 







Requirements (Must-Have):



Strong understanding of the english language, we will not tolerate mismatched footage, spelling errors etc. 

 At least 1 year of professional video editing experience (YouTube faceless or similar channels preferred)

Strong proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro (DaVinci Resolve or Final Cut Pro acceptable)

Strong English proficiency – you must fully understand scripts and content to choose appropriate visuals and timing

Ability to find and select high-quality, relevant stock footage/B-roll that matches the script and niche

Experience with or willingness to learn ElevenLabs for voiceover generation (big plus if you already have it)

Reliable high-speed internet and a powerful computer for smooth 1080p/4K editing

Excellent time management – able to deliver high-quality work on tight deadlines

Self-motivated, detail-oriented, and able to work independently with minimal supervision



Example of videos you would be creating: 











How to Apply (Important – Follow Instructions Carefully):



1. Provide links to 3–5 examples of your best edited YouTube-style videos (faceless channels preferred – include links with timestamps if possible).



2. In your application message, include:



• Your years of video editing experience

• Editing software you are strongest with

• Confirmation that you can adhere to 48 hour turnaround time for a 30 b roll video like provided in the examples above.

• Confirmation that you can source your own stock footage/B-roll

• Any experience with ElevenLabs or AI voice tools (optional but mention if you have it)



3. We will review portfolios and invite selected applicants to complete a paid test project ($10). You will receive a short raw sample (script + voiceover) and instructions to edit a 1-minute test video, including sourcing appropriate stock footage.



Only complete applications with strong portfolio examples will be considered. We need 3 editors to start immediately. 



If you’re a talented, reliable editor who can create engaging faceless videos and grow with us, apply now!

title: Facebook Sales & Lead Generation VA
description:
Commission-First Role

 Sales & Lead Generation VA

Part-time to full-time · Commission + hourly · Long-term



We're looking for a sharp, self-directed
 sales professional who knows how to start conversations, revive cold leads, and turn activity into revenue. This role is built for someone who doesn't need hand-holding — you take ownership, figure things out, and show up with results.



The hourly rate is intentionally modest. The real money is in commission — and if you're genuinely good at sales and
 outreach, you will earn very well here.



What you'll be doing

-Re-engaging cold and old leads through
 Messenger and 

-Starting new conversations and qualifying prospects

-Posting in relevant
 groups and growing our presence organically

-Running and managing
 Meta ad campaigns — you're comfortable reading numbers and optimizing for performance

-Staying on top of promotions, seasonal offers, and marketing opportunities to drive new leads



Who you are:



-You have real, proven experience with
 sales, social selling, or sales in general

-You've run or managed Meta ad campaigns and can speak to results (ROAS, CPL, CTR — these aren't new words to you)

-You're autonomous. You don't wait to be told what to do next — you identify the problem and solve it

-You communicate clearly and follow up without being asked

You're comfortable working independently and across time zones



What this is not:

-This is not a role where you'll be trained from scratch on sales basics

-There is no daily check-in or micromanagement — you're expected to operate independently

-If you need a script for every message or step-by-step instructions for every task, this isn't the right fit



Compensation:



-Base hourly rate (below market) + generous commission on every closed deal you contributed to. Top performers here earn significantly more than a standard VA wage. This structure is intentional — we want people who are motivated by results, not the clock.



How to apply

Send a short message that includes:



Your experience with
 sales or Meta ads (be specific — results, not just job titles)

One example of a campaign or outreach effort you ran and what the outcome was

Why a commission-first role appeals to you

title: Short Form Editor
description:
College Walk — Final Cut Video Editor

College Walk is a media agency producing street interview-style video ads for top consumer apps (clients ranked in the top 50 of their App Store category). We're hiring final cut editors to take rough cuts of campus interviews and turn them into polished, high-converting 9:16 ad creatives for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.

What you'll do:



Edit street interview footage into short-form vertical ads (15-60 seconds)

Reframe and crop horizontal source to 9:16, matching framing to the active speaker

Trim dialogue (remove ums, silences, disfluencies) and pace cuts for energy

Color correct, balance audio, and add karaoke-style captions

Layer in approved music, sound effects, and motion graphics from our asset library

Add product demos using StreetTalk-style phone mockup overlays



You're a fit if you:



Have experience editing short-form social ads (UGC, TikTok, Reels, Shorts)

Are proficient in CapCut, Premiere Pro, or DaVinci Resolve

Understand pacing for ad creatives — hooks, retention, punchlines

Can deliver multiple finished videos per day with fast turnaround

Communicate reliably and meet deadlines



Reference for our style: StreetTalk's Consumer Apps library

Ongoing high-volume work. Pay per video. Apply with portfolio samples of short-form ad work.

title: Executive Real Estate VA – Sales, STR Optimization & Operations (Long-Term & Short-Term Rental)

You aren't just managing tasks; you are optimizing revenue, securing new owners, and ensuring our operations run like a Swiss watch.



IMPORTANT: To show you have high attention to detail, you must start your response/cover letter with the words "Blue DOTs". Applications that do not include this will be automatically disqualified.



Key Responsibilities

STR Revenue Management: (Expert Level) Daily management of PriceLabs to optimize dynamic pricing and Rankbreeze to monitor and improve search rankings. You must understand RevPAR and occupancy optimization.



Listing Excellence: Create, launch, and optimize high-conversion listings across MLS, Airbnb, and major rental platforms.



The "Closer" Mindset: Handle outbound cold calling to property owners to grow the portfolio and manage fast-paced inbound inquiries 
 Messenger, Ads, and phone).



Virtual Transformation: Use Canva or virtual staging tools to turn empty rooms into high-end, appealing pictures.



Lease & Document Management (Top Priority): Manage drafting of lease agreements, move-in/out reports, and property documentation with 100?curacy.



Strategic Scheduling: Complete ownership of the calendar. Coordinate showings, manage vendor appointments, and ensure all current occupants receive proper legal notice for entry.



Requirements

Tech Stack: Proven experience with PriceLabs, Rankbreeze, and Property Management Software (Hostaway/Buildium experience is a plus).



English Proficiency: Exceptional verbal and written English for tenant screening and owner cold calls.



Accuracy: A "zero-error" mentality when it comes to legal dates and financial figures in lease documents.



Screening Questions (Answer These to be Considered)

STR Optimization: Describe your specific workflow using PriceLabs and Rankbreeze. How do you d
 when to drop a price versus when to hold for a higher-value booking?



Sales & Prospecting: Describe your experience with cold calling. How do you handle objections from property owners who are hesitant to use a manager?



Document Accuracy: We have zero tolerance for errors in lease agreements. What is your specific "double-check" process to ensure names, dates, and terms are 100% correct before sending for signature?



Calendar Management: How long have you managed a professional’s calendar, and what tools (e.g., Calendly, Google Calendar) are you an expert in?

title: Video Editor for Ecommerce - Veo3, Flow, Higgsfield, Sora
description:

Great editing is what turns good concepts into winning ads. We’re looking for a Video Editor who understands direct-response marketing and wants to get better at it. Not just someone who makes videos look pretty. Someone who understands how ads actually convert.

Every edit you make has a measurable impact. You'll see what performs, learn why, and use that insight to get sharper with every iteration.

- Testing variations of pacing, structure, and messaging

- Using AI tools to speed up production workflows

- Studying competitor ads and creative trends

**Compensation**

$1,500 – $2,500 per month depending on experience.

title: Appointment Setter: High-Ticket Sales (Facebook Outreach | Credit Repair Coaching)
description:
We are a coaching company that helps entrepreneurs launch and scale profitable 

credit repair businesses. We're looking for a driven, people-savvy appointment 

setter to generate qualified sales calls through organic outbound outreach on 

 Messenger.



This is not a cold calling role. You will be DMing business owners and 

entrepreneurs on
 who show interest in financial freedom, business 

ownership, or the credit industry, starting genuine conversations and booking 

them onto a sales call with our closer.



RESPONSIBILITIES:

- Prospect and identify ideal leads on
 (business owners, entrepreneurs, 

  side hustlers, people interested in credit repair or financial services)

- Initiate and manage organic outbound conversations via
 Messenger 

  using our proven scripts and frameworks

- Qualify prospects based on our criteria (business mindset, budget awareness, 

  desire to build income)

- Book qualified appointments directly onto the closer's calendar

- Follow up with leads who didn't show or need more nurturing

- Track all outreach activity and booked calls in our CRM or tracking sheet

- Maintain a consistent daily outreach volume (target: 50–100 new DMs/day)

- Communicate professionally and represent the brand with integrity at all times



COMPENSATION:

- Hourly base pay

- Commission paid per closed sale on every deal the closer converts from your 

  booked appointments

- High-ticket program, high quality leads on our calls means strong commissions 

  for you



REQUIREMENTS:

- Active
 presence and comfort navigating
 groups, profiles, 

  and Messenger

- Strong written English, you must be able to hold natural, warm conversations 

  that don't feel salesy

- Experience in appointment setting, outbound sales, or social media outreach 

  preferred

- Self-motivated with the ability to hit daily outreach targets independently

- Familiarity with the credit repair, financial services, or business coaching 

  space is a big plus

- Reliable internet connection and availability during US business hours 

  (EST or PST overlap required)

- Must be comfortable using a CRM or tracking spreadsheet to log activity



This role rewards performance. The more qualified appointments you book that 

close, the more you earn. We provide scripts, training, and ongoing support, 

you bring the consistency and the people skills.

title: Video Editor & Graphic Designer
description:
Location: Fully Remote (Must be based in the Philippines)



Job Type: Full-Time



Create scroll-stopping content for a scaling DTC brand. We need a performance-focused video editor who can turn raw footage into high-converting ads and social content - with creative freedom and direct feedback loops on what's working.



About Uproot Clean



Uproot Clean is a fast-growing, eco-friendly pet care brand expanding across major retailers like Walmart, Target, and Chewy. Our marketing team is scaling rapidly, and we're looking for a video editor who wants to grow their skills in performance marketing while building a long-term role with a mission-driven company.



The Role



You'll be our go-to editor for short-form video content that drives sales—think
/Instagram ads, TikTok content, and social clips. You'll work with a steady flow of creator and product footage, turning it into thumb-stopping content that performs. This isn't agency churn-and-burn; you'll see what converts, iterate based on data, and refine your craft with real-time feedback.



What You'll Do



Video Editing & Production

-Create high-performing
/Instagram ad creatives and social media clips

-Edit short-form content optimized for platform-specific formats

-Source and organize B-roll and stock footage using the provided subscriptions

-Manage creator content in 
 and maintain organized asset libraries



Graphic Design & Assets

-Design marketing images, thumbnails, and social graphics

-Create on-brand visual assets using Photoshop, Illustrator, or Canva



Project Management

-Track assignments and status updates in ClickUp

-Follow naming conventions and file organization protocols

-Communicate proactively about timelines, blockers, and questions





Requirements



3-5+ years of video editing experience, specializing in short-form content

Expert-level Adobe Premiere Pro skills (required)

Proven experience creating or editing
/Instagram ads

Mid-level proficiency with Photoshop, Illustrator, or Canva

Strong fundamentals in typography, color theory, composition, and motion graphics

Fluent English — excellent written and verbal communication

Self-managed — strong time management and ability to meet daily/weekly deadlines

Proactive communicator — asks questions early, flags issues before they become problems

Portfolio required — you must submit a portfolio showcasing your video editing work. Applications without portfolios will not be considered.





Nice to Have



Background in DTC or e-commerce brands

Experience with remote collaboration tools (Slack, ClickUp, 
, Notion)

Familiarity with performance marketing metrics and what makes ads convert





Tech Requirements



Computer: i7 processor (or equivalent) with 16GB RAM minimum



Internet: 100 Mbps download speed minimum



Schedule



Monday-Friday: 10:00 PM - 7:00 AM EST (1-hour break)



Required: Weekly tea
eting on Thursdays at 10:00 AM EST





Benefits & Perks



Fully remote - work from anywhere in the Philippines

Paid time off

Flex days. Enjoy an extra quarterly day off to unwind and recharge energies whenever you desire.

Professional development - opportunities to learn and grow with the business





Ready to Apply?



If you love creating high-performing video content and have a portfolio that showcases your work, we want to see it. Apply now.



Note: Applications without portfolio samples will not be reviewed.

title: AI Content Production Specialist (Part?Time, Long?Term)
description:
We are hiring a dedicated **AI Content Production Specialist** to help us generate a wide range of business, marketing, and operational assets using AI tools.



This role is **focused on AI execution**, not strategy. You will NOT be responsible for creating business ideas, offers, or positioning. Your role is to take structured instructions, prompts, and frameworks and efficiently produce high?quality outputs using AI.



This is a long?term, part?time role (approximately 16–20 hours per week).



---



### What You’ll Be Doing

You will use AI tools to produce content and assets such as:

-
 campaigns and sequences

- Website and landing page drafts

- SOPs, job descriptions, and internal documents

- Marketing and sales templates

- Lead magnets, guides, and structured outlines

- Social media and short?form marketing content



You will also:

- Edit, clean, and organize AI?generated output

- Follow detailed prompts and production frameworks

- Deliver assets in clean, ready?to?use formats (Google Docs, Slides, etc.)



This is **production work**, not brainstorming or strategy.



---



### Required Experience

- Ability to follow structured prompts and instructions precisely

---



### Nice to Have (Not Required)

 platforms (Mailchimp, HubSpot, GoHighLevel, etc.)

---

? Not a marketing strategist  

? Not a copywriting role  

? Not idea generation or consulting  



? This role is about **output, consistency, and execution using AI**



---



### Schedule & Pay

- Compensation based on experience using AI



Please include the words “AI?Operator” in your application subject.



### How to Apply

Applications that clearly demonstrate real AI usage will be prioritized.

title: HR Specialist (Remote – Philippines)
description:
Job Title: HR Specialist (Remote – Philippines)



Company: Eco Property Maintenance (Canada)



***LOOKING FOR CANDIDATES WITH EXPERIENCE WORKING WITH CANADIAN COMPANIES***



About the Role:

Eco Property Maintenance is a growing Canadian property maintenance company with ~25+ field staff. We are hiring a full-time HR Operations Administrator to own and execute our people processes — hiring support, onboarding, offboarding, employee records, and HR administration. You will work from clear SOPs, checklists, and scripts, and escalate decisions when required.



If you are highly organized, detail-oriented, confident communicating in English, and experienced supporting North American companies remotely — this role will be a great fit.



Key Responsibilities:



1. Hiring & Recruitment Support

-Maintain and post job ads across platforms (e.g. Indeed)

-Screen resumes against defined criteria

-Conduct initial screening calls with candidates

-Record call notes and candidate details in our HR system (BambooHR or tracker)

-Complete interviews / record to share with managers

-Track candidates through the hiring pipeline

-Maintain organized hiring records and status updates

-Coordinate trial days with zone managers



2. Onboarding (Primary Focus)

Send onboarding packages to new hires (contracts, tax forms, personal info forms, etc.)

-Track completion of all onboarding documents

-Maintain and follow onboarding checklists

-Ensure system access, documentation, and setup steps are completed

-Ensure every new hire receives a consistent onboarding experience



3. Offboarding

-Trigger and manage offboarding checklists

-Prepare and process ROEs

-Coordinate benefits cancellation

-Collect company property (documentation + tracking)

-Conduct exit interviews using provided scripts

-Log and categorize exit reasons for management review



4. HR Administration & Compliance Tracking

-Maintain up-to-date employee records

-Track required documents and certifications

-Monitor WCB-related documentation and requirements

-Flag missing or expired documents

-Escalate compliance issues to management when needed

(You will not interpret employment law — only track and report)



5. Employee Support & Engagement (Structured)

-Schedule and support periodic employee check-ins

-Assist with performance review scheduling

-Track goals and follow-ups for field staff

-Collect employee feedback using structured questions

-Summarize trends and insights for management



What This Role Is NOT:

-Making hiring or termination decisions

-Interpreting employment law or policies

-Handling employee conflicts independently

-Negotiating compensation or benefits

-Creating policy without approval

-You will execute processes, document outcomes, and escalate when guidance is needed.



Required Qualifications:

-2+ years experience in HR administration, recruitment support, or people operations

-Experience supporting North American (US or Canadian) companies

-Fluent spoken and written English (required)

-Comfortable running screening calls independently

-Strong attention to detail and follow-up

-Confident using checklists, trackers, and SOPs

-Reliable internet and professional remote setup



Preferred Qualifications:

-Experience working remotely with Canadian companies

-Familiarity with HR software (BambooHR or similar)

-Experience hiring blue-collar or field-based employees

-Prior VA or remote HR role



Tools You’ll Use:

-Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Drive)

-BambooHR (or similar HR system)

-Communication tools (whats app /
 / Zoom)



Compensation & Schedule:

-$900–$1,200 USD per month

-Full-time: 40 hours per week

-Long-term role with growth potential

-Paid trial period required

description:

title: Recruitment Appointment Setter| Full-Time | $1000
description:
We’re hiring a Recruitment Outreach & Appointment Setting Specialist to handle phone-based candidate engagement and help move applicants forward in our hiring process.



This role is focused on calling candidates, confirming interest, and booking interviews. It is not a strategy or management role; it’s a hands-on, execution-focused position.



• Pay: $1000/month

• Bonus: Performance-based bonus (details shared with qualified candidates)

• Schedule: Full-time, fixed Mountain Time hours (9am–5pm MT)

• Work Setup: Remote

• Commitment: Full-time only (no other active jobs)

title: Do you love making people feel cared for? Support our CEOs as an Executive Personal Assistant!
description:
Title: Executive Personal Assistant



Important: Make sure to read the full listing for application instructions.



Do you love making people feel cared for? Are you the one who remembers everyone’s birthdays, plans amazing gatherings, makes fun plans, and brings warmth into the lives of those around you?



This is NOT your typical assistant job. It’s deeply personal. You’ll work with a kind but intense U.S.-based pair of brothers (proven entrepreneurs working as Co-CEOs of the same business) to support their lives, not the company they are building. From keeping friendships alive and planning meaningful experiences to sending thoughtful gifts and helping them stay on track with personal goals, you’ll be part organizer, part cheerleader, part coach. If you’ve ever been called the “mom” of your group and you’re looking for a decades-long position, this might be your dream job.



Your Role

* Build trust by executing on tasks and communicating clearly, which will lead to taking on more responsibility, which will then lead to increases in pay.

* Keep relationships thriving by managing a personal CRM and helping stay in touch with friends and family.

* Use technology to help automate certain tasks that are repetitive.

* Track habits, support routines, and act as an accountability partner for personal goals.

* Pick out thoughtful, creative gifts for birthdays and other special 
nts.

* Research products, tools, and ideas to make life easier, better, or more fun.

* Plan and book unforgettable travel, great hotels, and the perfect dinner spots.

* Jump in on special personal projects wherever a helping hand is needed.



What’s In It for You

* A Long-Term Role: This is meant to last. The entrepreneurs who you'd be an assistant to are looking for a lifetime assistant.

* Great Place to Work: These entrepreneurs are known to have a super high retention rate of employees and create a great work environment.

* Creative Work: From planning memorable trips to picking out the perfect gift, this role lets you flex your creative muscles and thoughtful side every day.

* Variety: No two days will feel the same. You’ll get to research, plan, coach, organize, and more.

* Paid Leave: You'll get at least 10 days of paid leave plus major holidays.

* Remote Flexibility: Work from the comfort of your home and gain flexibility with hours once trust is built.



Desired Attributes

* Extremely caring, social people-person who will excel in personal tasks

* High attention to detail and organizational skills

* Very strong written and oral English skills

* Minimum 50 Mbps Internet Speed

* Designated office space in your home



To apply and ensure your application is considered, do all of the following two steps:

1. DM with a quick introduction to yourself if you're interested. Include the word "cupcake" *in your subject line*, and Include your resume. ++Bonus points for a video introduction, though it's not strictly required

2. Fill out this form:

3.  Add the Letters "PA" in the "What's your full name" field [E.g. PA - John Doe], so we know what role you are applying for.

title: Sales and Customer Care Specialist
description:
About Us: 

Brist Mfg is a custom apparel company who creates goods to be worn out not thrown out. This is an exciting opportunity to be part of a growing, dynamic team headquartered in Bellingham, Washington, USA.



Job Description

The project coordinator is a collaborative member of sales operations and is equipped to make sound supply chain decisions based on an understanding of customer budgets, product and delivery needs and the pricing and profitability of sales orders. Through the lens of sourcing, estimating and order management experience and expertise, the project coordinator actively seeks to enhance customer value and drive account retention to achieve vertical, department and company goals. 



Essential Responsibilities:

Manage all aspects of the order/project to ensure accurate communication, on time delivery and products meet or exceed client expectations.

Work with sales members to create estimates and quotes, navigate timelines that will lead to successful outcomes.

Process/ Assign and track customer orders, working closely with sales to ensure all information is translated accurately within the operating system, through supply and production channels.

Prepare quotes and estimates within expected deadlines.

Operate with a customer centric mindset that leads to successful outcomes.

Ensure all order information is translated accurately within the operating system, through supply and production channels.

Manage special requests regarding new products creation.

Manage order issues through solution positioning/costing in collaboration with the rep and directors. 

Update Sales Orders with the tech packs, delivery, shipping and costing information.

Effectively prioritize multiple projects with varying levels of urgency and timelines in a high-volume, interrupt-driven environment.

Be open to assisting others as needed, learning and performing applicable duties when called upon.



Required Skills/Abilities:

Proficient in math fundamentals

Fluent English (verbal and written) with effective communication skills 

Demonstrated ability to thrive in a fast-paced, constantly evolving work environment

Experience working in a collaborative decision making environment, reaching consensus to deliver the best outcomes possible

A Lean and continuous improvement mindset, can see opportunity to reduce waste and create customer value

Highly organized and detail-oriented

Flexible, ability to prioritize and manage changes throughout the workday, meet frequent shifts in schedules and customer demands

Curious, willing to learn and take on new responsibilities

Ability to learn/navigate functionality within operating systems 

Ability to quickly identify errors and solve problems

Good humored with a positive “can do” attitude, see the glass “half full vs. half empty”



Required Experience/Education:

1-2 years experience in a supply chain or sales support role

Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience

Demonstrated use and understanding of ERP software and operating systems, CRM, Google Sheets

Proficiency in Microsoft applications (Word, Excel, Outlook and PowerPoint)

Progressive advancement in learning, job duties and responsibilities within an organization
