title: Tenancy manager
description:
Job Title

Tenancy Management Admin – Full Time – Remote



Job Description

We are looking for a highly reliable, detail-oriented, and proactive Tenancy Manager Admin to join our team full time on a remote basis.



We manage a portfolio of residential properties in central London and need someone who is precise, organised, and able to handle important administrative tasks without mistakes. This is NOT a generic VA role. We need someone who can work carefully, follow procedures exactly, and take real ownership of their work.



This role involves handling sensitive tasks such as rent tracking, tenant administration, contract checks, move-in coordination, landlord and tenant communications, and general property management support (problem solving). Accuracy is absolutely essential.



Main Responsibilities



Update and maintain rent ledgers accurately

Track rent payments, arrears, and outstanding balances

Prepare and check tenancy documents, guarantor documents, and contract packs

Ensure all required documents and payments are complete before move-ins

Draft clear and professional
s to tenants, landlords, and contractors

Solve and deal with maintenance issues through our trusted contractors

Follow up on maintenance issues and confirm completion

Keep spreadsheets, records, and internal systems fully accurate and up to date

Report any issue, discrepancy immediately and handle general admin tasks

Follow procedures carefully and double/triple-check all important tasks before completion



Requirements



Previous experience in property management, real estate admin, leasing support, or a similar role (Uk or US preferred but not essential)

Excellent written and spoken English

Very strong attention to detail

Highly organised and able to manage multiple tasks carefully

Able to follow instructions and procedures exactly

Good problem-solving skills and common sense

Comfortable using spreadsheets,
, and online systems

Able to work independently and take ownership of tasks

Honest, reliable, and consistent, and able to work under pressure



Important

This role requires a high level of accuracy. Mistakes in this position can have financial and operational consequences. We are looking for someone who naturally double-checks their work and is able to think outside the box and takes pride in getting things right.



What We Offer

Full-time remote role

Opportunity to grow within the company

Performance-based company bonus

2 weeks Paid holidays after 1 year of continuous work

Long-term opportunity for the right candidate



Working Hours

Applicants must be available to work during UK business hours



Salary

Competitive, depending on experience and suitability.

Performance bonus



Application Process

To apply, please send:



Your CV

A short introduction about your experience in property management or similar work

Examples of the types of admin/property tasks you have handled

Confirmation of your availability for full-time work

Confirmation of whether you currently work for any other clients



Please note that shortlisted candidates will be asked to complete a practical test to assess accuracy, judgment, and attention 

to detail.



Please note: to confirm attention to detail, copy and paste this exact sentence at the top of your application: “I understand that accuracy is essential in tenancy management.”

title: Google Ads Waste Suppression Expert - Capital Recovery Specialist (500€/client)
description:
About AdsKore:

AdsKore is an elite Arbitrage Cabinet focusing on B2B Google Ads performance. We don't just "manage" ads; we hunt and destroy budget waste. We are looking for high-level "Snipers" to join our network of experts.

Your Mission:

Your only goal is to identify and eliminate the 20% to 30% of budget waste that Google's algorithm generates. You will be responsible for the "Capital Recovery" of our French B2B clients.

What you will do:

• Aggressive Negative Auditing: Scrutinize search terms daily to exclude non-converting, informational, or "junk" queries.

• Placement Cleaning: Block mobile apps and low-quality display placements that drain cash.

• B2B Intent Filtering: Use AI tools (DeepL/ChatGPT) to ensure the intent of French keywords is 100% commercial/transactional.

• Performance Reporting: Fill out our weekly "Capital Recovered" tracker to prove your value.

Requirements:

• Proven experience in Google Ads (B2B preferred).

• Data-obsessed mindset. You hate wasting even 1€.

• Fluent in English. Ability to use AI tools to navigate French accounts is a must.

• Experience with Optmyzr, Adalysis, or advanced Google Ads scripts is a plus.

Compensation:

500€ per month, per account managed.

We are looking for long-term partners. As we grow, you can manage multiple accounts and scale your income significantly.

How to apply:

Start your application with the word "CLEANER" so I know you are a detail-oriented pro. Tell me about the biggest "waste" you ever found in a Google Ads account.

title: Experienced Amazon PPC Manager
description:
We move fast. We're obsessed with skincare. And we need someone who eats, sleeps, and breathes Amazon PPC to join our team and take our advertising game to the next level.

If you're the kind of person who gets a rush from watching ACoS drop while sales climb - keep reading.



The Role

You'll own our entire Amazon advertising and sales strategy end to end. That means you're not just pushing buttons in a dashboard, you're the person who spots the opportunity everyone else missed, tests the idea no one else thought of, and delivers results that make the whole team look good.



What You'll Be Doing

- [ ] Mastering Scale Insights to squeeze every drop of performance out of our campaigns - from bid management to keyword research to ad creative testing. 

- [ ] Diving deep into the data, running A/B tests, and turning numbers into decisions that actually move the needle. 

- [ ] Managing budgets across multiple product lines with a sharp eye on ROI - every pound working harder than the last. 

- [ ] Building out PPC strategies across Sponsored Products, Brands, and Display that drive real visibility and real sales. 

- [ ] Writing punchy, scroll-stopping copy for Brand and Display ads. 

- [ ] Keeping a close watch on search performance and rankings across key channels. 

- [ ] Collaborating with our marketing team to make sure PPC and brand strategy are pulling in the same direction. 

- [ ] Staying ahead of the curve - you're the one telling us about platform changes and new tactics, not the other way around.



What You Bring

- [ ] Proven experience managing Amazon PPC campaigns with measurable results. 

- [ ] Deep fluency in Scale Insights, Helium10, Data Dive. 

- [ ] Hands-on experience with Flat Files for catalog uploads and edits.

- [ ] Strong analytical chops - you can take a messy data set and pull out a clear story. 

- [ ] A solid grasp of A/B and multivariate testing. 

- [ ] Knowledge of current e-commerce and digital advertising best practices. 

- [ ] Clear communication - you can explain what's working (and what's not) without the jargon.

title: Construction Admin VA (Remote)
description:
IMPORTANT: READ BEFORE APPLYING

We are only considering candidates with real construction project management experience (commercial preferred). If you do not have this experience, please do not apply.



About the Role

We are hiring a full-time Construction Admin Virtual Assistant to support commercial tenant improvement (TI) projects ranging from 5,000 to 10,000 square feet. This role supports the full project lifecycle, from pre-acquisition through Certificate of Occupancy and final handoff.



You will work closely with general contractors, subcontractors, lenders, and city officials to ensure projects stay organized, on schedule, and compliant.



What You’ll Be Doing

In this role, you will be responsible for managing RFPs and contractor bidding by creating scopes of work, handling multiple bids per trade, building comparison sheets, and verifying contractor compliance documents such as COIs and W-9s.



You will also oversee budgeting and draw management, including maintaining monthly draw schedules, reviewing AIA G702 pay applications, collecting lien waivers, coordinating with construction lenders, tracking budgets, and processing change orders.



A key part of your role will involve coordinating with general contractors and subcontractors by maintaining project schedules, tracking daily logs and trade sequencing, and managing punch lists to ensure smooth project execution.



You will support city and municipality coordination by conducting zoning research, assisting with permit submissions, tracking approvals, and scheduling inspections.



Additionally, you will handle documentation and administrative tasks such as organizing Google Drive folders, maintaining compliance trackers, providing weekly project updates, and updating SOPs as processes evolve.



Requirements (Non-Negotiable)

We are looking for someone with proven construction project management experience, preferably in commercial tenant improvement projects. You must be familiar with AIA G702 pay applications and lien waivers and have daily working experience using ClickUp, Slack, Google Drive, and AI tools such as ChatGPT or Claude.



Strong English communication skills, both written and verbal, are required, along with a high level of organization and attention to detail to manage strict deadlines and documentation standards.



Schedule & Compensation

This is a full-time role, Monday through Friday from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM CST. The pay ranges from $12 to $14 per hour, with a $1,000 bonus for each successfully completed project. You will also receive 10 PTO days per year.



How to Apply:

To apply, please attach your updated resume(PDF) and a 1–2 minute voice recording introducing yourself and your construction experience.

title: Customer Support Specialist
description:
We are seeking a highly skilled and empathetic Customer Support Specialist to handle complex, high-volume user inquiries and technical issues for our platform. This role goes beyond basic troubleshooting and requires a strong technical mindset, including the use of SQL, APIs, and diagnostic tools to investigate and resolve production issues. You will support non-profit customers, organizers, and donors by triaging unexpected platform behavior, navigating sensitive user situations, and collaborating closely with Engineering and Product teams to ensure timely and effective resolutions. The ideal candidate is analytical, tech-savvy, and compassionate, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.



Key Responsibilities:



Investigate and resolve complex user issues that extend beyond basic troubleshooting using SQL queries, APIs, and internal tools.



Perform technical triage through
 and phone support, analyzing datasets and platform behavior to diagnose root causes.



Manage high-volume support tickets related to accounts, platform functionality, and bank transfer verifications with professionalism and empathy.



Document and investigate “unexpected behavior” within a 45-minute window before escalation.



Write clear, detailed JIRA tickets including API values, console error logs, reproduction steps, and database update tracking.



Collaborate cross-functionally with Engineering and Product teams to escalate bugs, advocate for user needs, and drive issue resolution.



Optimize workflows, documentation, and self-service resources to scale support operations and improve user experience.



Support non-profit customers, organizers, and donors while navigating sensitive and emotionally complex situations.



Troubleshoot software application issues and integrations in real-time using technical tools and diagnostic methods.



Monitor and meet key performance metrics such as CSAT, SLAs, and resolution time targets.



Required Qualifications:



2–5 years of experience in a customer-facing product or technical support role, with a strong focus on troubleshooting technical issues (1–3 years in customer service or help desk environments will also be considered for high-volume support experience).



Proficiency in SQL for data querying, analysis, and root cause investigation.



Hands-on experience working with APIs and understanding technical integrations.



Basic understanding of programming concepts and software application troubleshooting.



Experience with case management and structured issue documentation.



Strong experience with ticketing systems and writing technical reports (e.g., JIRA or similar platforms).



Core Competencies:



Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate technical concepts for non-technical users.



Advanced problem-solving skills and the ability to independently diagnose and resolve complex issues.



High emotional intelligence and empathy, especially when supporting users facing sensitive or real-life challenges.



Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple high-priority tickets simultaneously.



Tech-savvy and quick to learn new tools, platforms, and internal systems.



Benefits:**

Fully remote:

Salary - $4 per hour X 40 hours per week (salary every Friday)

Opportunities for professional development and growth.

Dynamic and supportive work environment.

Monday to Friday, 9AM - 5PM EST

Job Security and Stability

PTO

Inclusive Culture

Upskilling Opportunities

100% Work-From-Home

Exceptionally Supportive Team

Opportunities for Career Growth

Technical Requirements:

USB Headset with Noise Cancellation feature

Working Webcam

Computer with at least 1.8 GHz processor and at least 4GB RAM

Main Internet Service Speed: at least 25 Mbps cable connection

Backup Internet Service Speed: at least 10 Mbps



Please click the link below to apply:

title: Social Media Manager | Video Editor
description:
Social Media Manager | Video Editor | Graphic Designer

DISCLAIMER: Interested parties must send their applications and inquiries ONLY to 
 unless advised otherwise. Non-compliance herewith shall negatively affect your application.



I. Summary



Our firm is seeking a detail-oriented and creative fully remote full-time in-house Social Media manager to oversee content ideation, planning, development and execution across digital platforms. The role focuses on end-to-end content production including administrative tasks such as analytics, reporting and content calendar management.



The ideal candidate has one (1) to two (2) years’ experience of managing business social media accounts, a familiarity with brand management and platform specific content as well as working knowledge of analytics software. To emphasize, knowledge of video and graphic editing tools is a strict requirement.  This position requires a results-driven, proactive and independent professional who can showcase creativity while working within the bounds of the demands of the brand.



II. Responsibilities

  A. Analytics and Reporting

   1. Monitor Social Media Engagement and Conversions



                2. Deliver regular reports with Meta Insights and Google Analytics on a weekly and monthly basis.

   

                3. Adjust strategy based on performance



  B. Content Creation and Curation



1. Collaborate with end-to-end Production of Video and Graphic Content consistent with the client's branding



2. Customize messaging and format to align with individual platform requirements while maximizing the content's reach and engagement.



3. Execute client-requested revisions with efficient turnaround times.



  C. Content Calendar Management 



1. Create and adjust data-driven content calendars aligned with strategic marketing objectives

   

2. Optimize posting times based on existing performance metrics



3. Ensure consistent publishing cadence to strengthen brand visibility, audience retention and conversion.



III. Qualifications



A. Education and Experience



1. One (1) to two (2) years’ experience in social media management or digital marketing. Candidates with less than one (1) year experience may be considered, provided they are able to demonstrate the capability to perform the role effectively and efficiently.



2. Experience managing at least one (1) to two (2) business accounts engaged in the legal industry and/or high end, luxury or exclusive brands are preferred but not required.

   

3. Experience working in a remote environment is a plus.



4. A degree in Communications, Advertising or Marketing is preferred but not required.

   

5. Please prepare a portfolio of sample work.



   B. Skills



   1. Technical Skills



    a. Working knowledge of Meta Insights and Google Analytics



b. Familiarity with creating simple weekly and monthly performance reports



c. Familiarity with video editing tools. Advanced video editing tools are preferred but not required. No issues with basic video editing tools being used so long as the output meets brand standards.



d. Familiarity with Google Suite, Microsoft Office or other Calendar Management Software



    2. Soft Skills



    a. Time management

    

b. Self-starter requiring minimal supervision

    

c. Adaptability to high-paced environment

    

d. Receptive to feedback and revisions

    

e. Good Communication Skills



IV. Application Requirements  



A. Resume / CV



B. Portfolio (for video samples, please provide a link) 



C. One to two (1-2) minute introduction discussing why you are a good fit for the position.



D. Cover Letter (optional)



IV. Compensation and Working Hours



A. Hourly Rate: 5 USD / hr.  



B. Working Hours: 40 hrs. (8:30 AM – 4:30 PM) EST

 

C. Alternative work arrangements may be discussed after six (6) months upon demonstrating ability to meet deadlines.

title: Accounts Payable Specialist
description:
LED Trucking Inc., managed by Sacramento Managing Company, is seeking a highly detail-oriented and operationally disciplined Accounts Payable Specialist (Independent Contractor) to support financial operations with a primary focus on driver settlements, transaction processing, and account reconciliation. This role is structured as an independent contractor engagement and is responsible for delivering defined financial and operational outputs within a high-volume, asset-based transportation environment. The contractor will operate with a high level of autonomy while adhering to company-defined processes, timelines, and quality standards. The position reports functionally to the Head of Finance Division for workflow alignment and deliverables oversight.



 Job Responsibilities: 

Perform end-to-end driver settlement calculations in accordance with company policies and contractual structures

Manage communication with drivers regarding settlement inquiries, discrepancies, and issue resolution

Enter and maintain financial transactions within QuickBooks and TMS systems with a high degree of accuracy

Execute account control functions including transaction matching, verification, and closing of accounts

Maintain categorization and documentation of financial transactions in compliance with internal standards

Prepare and provide reports related to settlements, account activity, and financial records upon request

Identify discrepancies, escalate issues, and support resolution across Finance and Operations

Maintain audit-ready documentation and comply with internal financial controls and procedures

Perform additional accounting-related tasks tied to settlements and financial recordkeeping as required

All other duties, as assigned





Talent Requirements:

Minimum of two (2) years prior experience in accounts payable, accounting operations, or financial administration (transportation/logistics preferred)

Strong working knowledge of QuickBooks and transactional accounting processes

Experience with TMS platforms and high-volume financial environments preferred

High attention to detail and ability to maintain accuracy under volume

Strong organizational and time-management skills with the ability to meet defined deadlines

Effective communication skills, particularly in handling driver-facing financial discussions

Ability to independently manage workload while adhering to defined processes and KPIs

Contractor must maintain their own work environment, equipment, and connectivity

Strong general business acumen

Able to demonstrate professional phone etiquette

Demonstrate a high customer service commitment

Strong analytical skills – ability to assess, design, and sell a given solution

Ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously and maintain composure under pressure in a fast paced environment

title: Pre-Litigation Case Manager
description:
Responsibilities



Client Communication:

Serve as the primary point of contact for clients during the pre-litigation phase

Communicate regularly with clients to provide case updates, gather information and address inquiries.

Case Evaluation:

Conduct detailed reviews of personal injury cases to assess liability, damages, and potential legal strategies.

Work closely with attorneys to develop case strategies and action plans

Document Management:

Organize and manage case documentation, including medical records, police reports, and other relevant evidence.

Ensure all required documents are obtained and properly maintained.

Insurance Communication:

Communicate with insurance companies, opposing parties, and other involved entities to negotiate settlements and resolve pre-litigation matters.

Coordinate the exchange of information between parties involved in the case.

Medical Records Coordination:

Request, review, and organize medical records and bills related to clients' injuries.

Collaborate with medical providers to obtain necessary documentation.

Client Advocacy:

Advocate on behalf of clients to ensure their rights and interests are protected during the pre-litigation process.

Work towards fair and timely resolutions for clients.

Settlement Negotiations:

Engage in settlement negotiations with insurance adjusters to achieve favorable outcomes for clients.

Present comprehensive demand packages supported by relevant documentation.

Case Progression Tracking:

Utilize case management systems to track and document case progression.

Provide regular updates to attorneys and other stakeholders on case status.

Qualifications



Previous experience as a Case Manager or similar role in personal injury law.

Knowledge of personal injury law, case management processes, and pre-litigation procedures.

Strong negotiation and communication skills.

Detail-oriented with excellent organizational and time management abilities.

title: Odoo Systems Expert— Part-Time VA Click ACS · Westbury Enterprises Pty Ltd Remote | Part-Time | Monday to Friday | AEST
description:
About Us

Click ACS is a nationally scaling, majority Aboriginal-owned aged care services provider based in Port Macquarie, NSW. We support approximately 756 active clients across eight regions through domestic assistance, garden maintenance, and social transport services.

Odoo is our single source of truth. It runs our task management, client records, systemization, reporting, and team operations. As we scale, we need someone who can make it work harder.



The Role

This is a hands-on, execution-focused Odoo role. You will work directly with our Admin Manager and Tech Lead to configure, optimise, and automate our Odoo environment. We are not looking for a consultant — we need someone who builds, documents, and delivers.



What You Will Do



Configure and maintain Odoo modules across CRM, Contacts, Projects, Planning, Documents, Knowledge, E-learning, Purchase, and Discuss

Build workflow automations, automated task reminders, and activity tracking

Design and maintain dashboards and reports for operational visibility

Support data migration and deduplication from ServiceM8 and Google Sheets into Odoo

Support integration work with third-party platforms (primarily ServiceM8)

Document every build in Odoo Knowledge — if it only works because you're holding it, it's not finished

Proactively flag gaps, limitations, and automation opportunities — we do not wait to be told





Must-Have Technical Skills



Odoo 17, 18, or 19 Enterprise Edition — hands-on, not just familiarity

Demonstrated experience across: CRM, Contacts, Projects, Planning, Documents, Knowledge, E-learning, Purchase, and Discuss

Automation and workflow setup within Odoo

Reporting and dashboard configuration and maintenance

Automated task reminders and activity tracking setup

Strongly Preferred



Odoo 19 Enterprise specific experience

Integration experience with ServiceM8 or similar field service software

Data migration and deduplication experience

Background supporting a services business





Practical Requirements

Part-time, Monday to Friday

Minimum 4 hours overlap with AEST business hours daily

Responsive on Slack and Odoo during overlap hours



Portfolio or examples of Odoo work built or maintained — required with application





How to Apply

Send your application to 
 with the subject line: Odoo Systems Expert— [Your Name]

title: Pre Construction Officer
description:
The Pre-Construction & Estimating Manager is responsible for overseeing the full pre-construction process—from initial estimates and takeoffs to plan check approvals. This role ensures projects are accurately scoped, budgeted, and fully prepared before construction begins.



Key responsibilities include preparing detailed cost estimates, reviewing plans and designs for buildability and code compliance, coordinating with architects, engineers, and consultants, and managing permit and plan check processes. The role also supports the sales team with accurate budgets and scopes while maintaining clear communication across all stakeholders.



The ideal candidate has strong residential construction experience, excellent attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced, remote environment.



Send your resume to 
 - remove the spaces.

title: Accounts Payable Specialist
description:
LED Trucking Inc., managed by Sacramento Managing Company, is seeking a highly detail-oriented and operationally disciplined Accounts Payable Specialist (Independent Contractor) to support financial operations with a primary focus on driver settlements, transaction processing, and account reconciliation. This role is structured as an independent contractor engagement and is responsible for delivering defined financial and operational outputs within a high-volume, asset-based transportation environment. The contractor will operate with a high level of autonomy while adhering to company-defined processes, timelines, and quality standards. The position reports functionally to the Head of Finance Division for workflow alignment and deliverables oversight.



 Job Responsibilities: 

Perform end-to-end driver settlement calculations in accordance with company policies and contractual structures

Manage communication with drivers regarding settlement inquiries, discrepancies, and issue resolution

Enter and maintain financial transactions within QuickBooks and TMS systems with a high degree of accuracy

Execute account control functions including transaction matching, verification, and closing of accounts

Maintain categorization and documentation of financial transactions in compliance with internal standards

Prepare and provide reports related to settlements, account activity, and financial records upon request

Identify discrepancies, escalate issues, and support resolution across Finance and Operations

Maintain audit-ready documentation and comply with internal financial controls and procedures

Perform additional accounting-related tasks tied to settlements and financial recordkeeping as required

All other duties, as assigned





Talent Requirements:

Minimum of two (2) years prior experience in accounts payable, accounting operations, or financial administration (transportation/logistics preferred)

Strong working knowledge of QuickBooks and transactional accounting processes

Experience with TMS platforms and high-volume financial environments preferred

High attention to detail and ability to maintain accuracy under volume

Strong organizational and time-management skills with the ability to meet defined deadlines

Effective communication skills, particularly in handling driver-facing financial discussions

Ability to independently manage workload while adhering to defined processes and KPIs

Contractor must maintain their own work environment, equipment, and connectivity

Strong general business acumen

Able to demonstrate professional phone etiquette

Demonstrate a high customer service commitment

Strong analytical skills – ability to assess, design, and sell a given solution

Ability to handle multiple tasks simultaneously and maintain composure under pressure in a fast paced environment

title: Remote Appointment Setter – Travel Industry
description:
We are hiring remote appointment setters to present and sell exclusive travel experiences for a US-based vacation program. If you’ve been wanting a role where you can work comfortably from home, save on rising daily expenses, avoid long commutes, and still grow your income, this could be a great fit for you.



Who This Is For



This role may be a great fit if you:

-Enjoy talking to people and making genuine connections

-Take pride in doing your work well, even when working independently

-Are looking for a long-term remote opportunity with growth potential

-Appreciate the flexibility of being at home while still staying productive





Requirements

-Excellent spoken English

-Friendly and confident communication skills on the phone

-Own computer and stable internet (minimum 25mbps)

-Quiet and professional workspace

-Ability to work US business hours

-Comfortable learning and using CRM systems

-Goal-oriented and willing to improve consistently





Responsibilities

-Reach out to warm leads via call,
, and text

-Help prepare prospects for their scheduled presentations

-Follow up with no-shows in a professional and helpful way

-Keep CRM records updated after each interaction





Preferred Experience

-Sales or telemarketing

-BPO or call center background

-Travel or hospitality industry experience





Compensation

-Training Period (First 3-4 Months)

US$400/month training allowance

Paid bi-monthly

Monthly evaluation based on participation, effort, and progress

-After Training 

Earn commission, no cap

OTE: US$1,200/month

Our top performers earn US$2,500+





Work Schedule

-26–27 business days per month (option to work more for higher earnings)

-Monday to Friday

8:00 AM – 5:00 PM EST (8:00 PM – 5:00 AM PHL) or 

12:00 PM - 9:00 PM EST (12:00 MN - 9:00 AM PHL)





Why Many People Appreciate This Role

-Work from the comfort of your home

-Save time and money by avoiding daily travel

-Be more available for family and personal responsibilities

-Build a steady income with room to grow

As you become more consistent and confident in the role, you’ll also gain more flexibility in how you manage your time—while still meeting your goals.





Application Process

Please submit:

-Resume (highlighting relevant experience)

-3 character references from previous jobs

-Short voice or video recording introducing yourself (Use Loom or Vocaroo)

-Internet speed test (Send link from Speedtest.net)





Answer these questions without AI–if you do, we will know when we speak to you:

-How would you handle a customer who says they are interested but want to think about it?

-How do you create urgency without sounding pushy?

-How would you respond if someone says they don’t trust online offers?

-How do you stay motivated when sales are slow?

-Have you ever missed a target? What did you learn?

-Why do you want to work remotely, and what motivates you?





Shortlisted applicants will be invited to a video interview and a short role-play scenario. If you’ve read this far, include “Aquarius” in your
 subject line and mention it in your voice/video recording.



Application Deadline: April 20, 2026

Training starts on: April 27, 2026

title: Paid Ads & Social Media Specialist
description:
Do you live and breathe Google Ads and Meta campaigns? Can you turn ad spend into actual leads — not just clicks? Do you know your way around a conversion funnel?



Innovest, a leading financial advice company in New Zealand, is looking for an experienced Paid Ads & Social Media Specialist to take ownership of our digital advertising and social presence. We help Kiwi families buy homes, protect their futures, and build wealth. Your job? Make sure the right people find us at the right time.

/Instagram) ad campaigns focused on lead generation

Bonus Points For:

• Experience with financial services, real estate, or professional services

• Video editing skills (Reels, Stories content)

• Experience with Australia/New Zealand audiences



What We Offer:

• Up to NZD$2,000.00 per month (based on experience)

• Permanent work from home

• Real ad budget to work with (NZD 5,000+/month)

• All tools provided — Canva Pro, scheduling tools, analytics

• Stable company that's been operating since 2006

• You'll be joining existing Filipino tea
mbers — not working solo



To Apply:

Please submit:

1. Your CV/resume to 
 with subject line “Paid Ads & Social Media Specialist – [YourName]”

We review applications as they come in — apply now!

title: SEO & Content Specialist
description:

Innovest, a leading financial advice company in New Zealand, is looking for an experienced SEO & Content Specialist to join our growing team. We help Kiwi families buy their first homes, protect their families with insurance, and build wealth through smart financial decisions. Now we need someone to help more people find us online.

Nice to Have:

What We Offer:

• Stable company (we've been around since 2006)

To Apply:

We review applications as they come in — don't wait to apply!

title: : AI-Powered Lead Gen Rockstar & Airbnb growth manager
description:
Job Title

Salary: $250 USD / Month + Uncapped Bonuses ($5 per "closed" lead)

The Mission: Help me build a multi-million dollar empire across Property Management and Social Lead Generation. I’m from London, and I’m looking for a partner who wants to learn the "London Way"



high-level English, high-level charisma, and high-level results.



The Schedule (London Time):



Mon–Fri: 3 hours/day (Evening shift)



Sunday: 1 hour "Quick-fire" inbox clearing.



Total: 16 hours of high-intensity work per week.



The Role: 50% Growth / 50?min



The "Dating Assistant" Role (Primary): You will manage Hinge profiles like a pro. You’ll use DeepSeek and other AI tools to A/B test which photos and openers get the best response. We are going to build an automated machine where you screenshot conversations, optimize the English, and secure dates.



The "Airbnb Specialist" Role: You’ll learn my systems for Airbnb and Booking.com. You’ll be responsible for uploading new listings and ensuring every guest feels like they’re staying in a 5-star hotel. Using a property channel management tools.



The "Daily Strategist": You are my right hand. You plan my day, use AI to automate your own tasks, and keep me "one step ahead" of everything.



Why You?



You are obsessed with AI. You don’t just use it; you want to see how far it can go.



You have a Sales Mindset. You don't just "message"; you "close."



You are Very Organized. You hate mess and love systems.



You have Ownership. If an Airbnb listing needs a 5-minute fix on a Saturday, you jump on it because you care about the result.



The "Billion Dollar" Perks:



Direct Coaching: I will personally coach you on high-level British English and AI prompting.



Bonuses: I want you to make money when I make money. Every successful appointment you set comes with a cash bonus.



HOW TO APPLY:



Tell me about the hardest project you have done online,
